text: Welcome to the Mind Expander Courage TV stream! We're about to get weird.
This voice you're hearing? It's a clone. Trained on just sixty-six audio clips, running through VoxCPM2 with custom LoRA weights.
The whole pipeline goes like this. You write a script, feed it to the model, and it generates speech in real-time. No pre-recording needed.
We trained the voice on RunPod using a custom dataset called M1ND3XPAND3RS Voice. Then deployed the inference endpoint on Modal with an A100 eighty gigabyte GPU.
The diffusion model runs ten steps with classifier-free guidance at two point zero. That gives us clean, natural speech at one point five times faster than real-time.
And now we've got this sick Courage the Cowardly Dog TV overlay running in OBS. Scan lines, VHS glitches, dust particles, the whole vibe.
The plan is to play public domain cartoons on the screen while I comment over them in real-time. Like MST3K but with an AI voice clone.
Total cost to run this? About two bucks twenty-six cents per hour of generated audio. Well under the five dollar budget we set.
Everything is open source. The fork, the LoRA training pipeline, the Modal endpoint, and now this stream overlay. All on GitHub under The Mind Expansion Network.
This is what happens when you combine voice cloning, diffusion models, and a cartoon about a purple dog living in the middle of nowhere. Return the slab!
